gene_description: Transcription Factor 7 Like 2 — a transcription factor involved in incretin signaling and glucose homeostasis. The rs7903146 variant is the strongest genetic risk factor for type 2 diabetes identified to date.

source.title: TCF7L2 Genetic Variation Augments Incretin Resistance and Influences Response to a Sulfonylurea and Metformin: The Study to Understand the Genetics of the Acute Response to Metformin and Glipizide in Humans (SUGAR-MGH)
source.finding: TCF7L2 rs7903146 C>T variant affects metformin and sulfonylurea response through incretin signaling. T allele carriers show improved glycemic response to metformin with 6.7-16.8% lower fasting glucose and 25.9% lower fasting insulin after treatment.

CC.phenotype: Higher type 2 diabetes risk, variable drug response
CC.effect: Wild-type genotype associated with increased type 2 diabetes risk. Suboptimal metformin response with modest glycemic improvement. Moderate sulfonylurea response expected.
CC.recommendation: Standard metformin and sulfonylurea dosing. Monitor HbA1c response closely. May require combination therapy or early addition of second agent if inadequate control.

CT.phenotype: Intermediate diabetes risk, enhanced drug response
CT.effect: Heterozygous T allele carriers show intermediate type 2 diabetes genetic risk. Improved glycemic response to both metformin and sulfonylureas. Better fasting glucose and insulin control compared to CC carriers. Approximately 6.7% lower fasting glucose after 6 months metformin therapy.
CT.recommendation: Standard dosing appropriate. Expect robust glycemic response to metformin and sulfonylureas. Good candidates for monotherapy trials before combination therapy needed.

TC.phenotype: Intermediate diabetes risk, enhanced drug response
TC.effect: Heterozygous T allele carriers show intermediate type 2 diabetes genetic risk. Improved glycemic response to both metformin and sulfonylureas. Better fasting glucose and insulin control compared to CC carriers. Approximately 6.7% lower fasting glucose after 6 months metformin therapy.
TC.recommendation: Standard dosing appropriate. Expect robust glycemic response to metformin and sulfonylureas. Good candidates for monotherapy trials before combination therapy needed.

TT.phenotype: Reduced diabetes risk, optimal drug response
TT.effect: Homozygous T allele carriers (about 25-30% of populations) show the strongest protection against type 2 diabetes and most dramatic drug response improvements. Approximately 16.8% lower fasting glucose and 25.9% lower fasting insulin after metformin treatment. Enhanced incretin signaling contributes to superior glycemic response.
TT.recommendation: Excellent candidates for metformin monotherapy with expected robust HbA1c reduction. Strong sulfonylurea responders. Prioritize lifestyle modifications as metabolic capacity is preserved. Earlier remission of diabetes possible.
